1343.reason: [This check uses tokens.get().getLine() which gets the line of the next token, not the last consumed token. When there's a missing RPARENT, it should report the line of the last valid token before the expected RPARENT]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: When checking for missing ')', uses tokens.get().getLine() which gets the line of the current token (which may be the next token after the missing ')'), but the error line should be the line of the last non-terminal before the missing ')', which is the line of the '(' token. Should use tokens.getLast().getLine() instead.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: When checking for missing ')', the line number used is tokens.get().getLine(), which is the line of the current token (the next token after the missing ')'), not the line of the last non-terminal before the missing ')'. According to the problem description, the error line number should correspond to the line where the last non-terminal before the right parenthesis is located. In this case, for the missing ')' in "ch = getchar( ;", the last non-terminal before the ')' is 'getchar', which is on line 16, but tokens.get() after consuming '(' would be the next token (e.g., ';'), which is on line 16 as well, but the logic is flawed because it should use the line of the last consumed token (the '(') or the function name. However, the main issue is that the error is reported correctly for line 16, but the code also has a duplicate consume of ASSIGN on line 18 (see next comment).
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [This check uses tokens.get().getLine() which gets the line of the next token, not the last consumed token. When there's a missing RPARENT, it should report the line of the last valid token before the expected RPARENT]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [This uses tokens.get().getLine() which gets the line of the next token, but should use tokens.getLast().getLine() to get the line of the last consumed token before the missing RPARENT]

1346.reason: Extra ASSIGN consume; after GetStmt handling, this should not be there.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: This line consumes an extra ASSIGN token. In the getint/getchar case, the ASSIGN was already consumed before the if block, so this line incorrectly consumes another ASSIGN, causing the parser to skip the actual assignment operator in subsequent statements.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: This line consumes an extra ASSIGN token. In the getint/getchar case, the ASSIGN was already consumed before the if block, so this line incorrectly consumes another ASSIGN, causing the parser to skip the next token (e.g., the ';' or the next expression). This leads to incorrect parsing and missing semicolon errors.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  Extra ASSIGN consume; after GetStmt handling, this should not be there.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [重复消耗了一个 ASSIGN 符号，导致后续解析出错]

1409.reason: [This uses tokens.get().getLine() which gets the line of the next token, but should use tokens.getLast().getLine() to get the line of the last consumed token before the missing RPARENT]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: Same issue as above - uses tokens.get().getLine() instead of tokens.getLast().getLine() for the missing ')' error line number.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: Similar to the first error, when checking for missing ')', the line number used is tokens.get().getLine(), which is the line of the current token after the missing ')', not the line of the last non-terminal before the missing ')'. For the missing ')' in "printf("%c",s[0];", the last non-terminal before the ')' is 's[0]' or the ';', but the correct line should be 19 (the line of the printf statement). The current code uses tokens.get() which after consuming the last comma and expression would be the next token (e.g., ';'), which is on line 19, so it happens to be correct in this case, but the logic is still fragile.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [This uses tokens.get().getLine() which gets the line of the next token, but should use tokens.getLast().getLine() to get the line of the last consumed token before the missing RPARENT]

1416.reason: The parser returns null without consuming the current token when encountering an unexpected token type. This causes the calling loop (e.g., in parseBlock) to repeat indefinitely with the same unconsumed token, resulting in a Time Limit Exceeded error.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The parser returns null without consuming the current token when encountering an unexpected token type. This causes the calling loop (e.g., in parseBlock) to repeat indefinitely with the same unconsumed token, resulting in a Time Limit Exceeded error.

1093.reason: The parser returns null without consuming the current token when encountering an unexpected token (like ')'). This causes the calling function (e.g., parseBlock) to loop indefinitely on the same token, leading to Time Limit Exceeded.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The parser returns null without consuming the current token when encountering an unexpected token (like ')'). This causes the calling function (e.g., parseBlock) to loop indefinitely on the same token, leading to Time Limit Exceeded.
